programme.title: God And Country
programme.desc: This documentary looks at the implications of Christian Nationalism and how it distorts not only America's constitutional republic, but Christianity itself. What happens when a faith built on love, sacrifice, and forgiveness is manipulated by some to have political tentacles, conflating power, money, and belief into hyper-nationalism?

programme.title: Words Matter
programme.desc: Mikmaw poet and published author Rebecca Thomas uses words for a living, but she can't speak the language stolen from her father at residential school.

programme.title: On Country Kitchen
programme.desc: Mark and Derek learn about the overabundance of sea urchin, and explore a new type of sustainable farming in Berrima; the boys have a go at beekeeping.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: Adam is joined in the kitchen by chefs Tom Walton and Brent Savage to take ordinary recipes and make them extraordinary.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: The Athenian orator Demosthenes seems to know the way to Arkadia. But Maxagaze, who wants to learn public speaking in hopes of winning the next pirate elections, needs his services.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities Of Gold
programme.desc: The original 80s animation classic that follows a young orphan called Esteban as he searches the New World for both his father and the mysterious Cities of Gold.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities Of Gold
programme.desc: The original 80s animation classic that follows a young orphan called Esteban as he searches the New World for both his father and the mysterious Cities of Gold.

programme.title: Little J & Big Cuz
programme.desc: Big Cuz doubts she's got what it takes to captain the school's rugby team.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: Uptight Everythings Alright And Agent Double O Soul
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: Harlem Globetrotters: Play It Forward
programme.desc: The Globetrotters recount the history of their beloved \.

programme.title: Garma: Celebrating 25 Years
programme.desc: Stories from Yolngu Country celebrating 25 years of Garma.

programme.title: The 77 Percent
programme.desc: Africa is home to a large number of youth as they constitute 77 percent of the continent's population. A few ambitious youngsters come together to share their vision for the continent's future.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: From Cambodia's diverse wildlife to people living in the stilt villages of lake Tonle Sap, the Mekong has dictated the lives of everything that lives there for centuries.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are? UK
programme.desc: Filmmaker Marc Fennell journeys to Singapore, Malaysia, and Ireland, uncovering his Indian roots and a hidden love story set during the Irish War of Independence.

programme.title: Living Black
programme.desc: Karla Grant travels to Western Australia to meet the rappers behind 'Banksia Beats', a hip-hop therapy program that's helping young detainees at Banksia Hill and Unit 18 find their voice through rap.

programme.title: Hidden Volcano: Eruption From The Abyss
programme.desc: January 2022, the volcano Hunga Tonga erupts. It is the most powerful explosion ever recorded. Within minutes waves as high as a six-storey building, devastate the Pacific nation of Tonga.

programme.title: The Judge
programme.desc: Years after he returns to his hometown, Chicago-based lawyer Hank Palmer decides to fight the case for his father, Joseph, an adjudicator, who is accused of murder.

programme.title: Stompem Ground 2022 - Footprince
programme.desc: A five-piece band line up from Broome, Footprince.

programme.title: Big Backyard Quiz
programme.desc: Team captains Elaine Crombie and Dave Woodhead as they put their Aussie knowledge to the test with guest panellists Rove McManus, Anisa Nandaula, Urvi Majumdar and Claire Hooper.

programme.title: NITV On The Road
programme.desc: From our travelling music series, this program showcases veterans and newcomers alike as they perform at the Barunga Festival 2016.

programme.title: Stompem Ground
programme.desc: With their large following in the Pilbara and Kimberley. Rock/Reggae family band Seaside Drifters keep it going with original songs including Wanamulnong, Little Sister, Reggae Me.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the long weekend. It is run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, and is a mixture of Australian and international Indigenous acts.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: This episode showcases beautiful Arrernte and Warlpiri Country, with locations such as Mparntwe Alice Springs and the Ellery Creek Big Hole.

programme.title: Toi Time
programme.desc: The Toi Time whanau celebrate Oma's birthday. Aunty Anika bakes the birthday cake and Buzz makes a beautiful bunch of putiputi for Oma.

programme.title: Waabiny Time
programme.desc: Keny, Koodjal, Dambart-One, Two Three. Counting is moorditj And do you know the kala, the colours of the rainbow.

programme.title: Bird's Eye View
programme.desc: The birds decide to get away from the noise of the city. Unfortunately, the Humans have the same idea. What are they doing now?

programme.title: Aussie Bush Tales
programme.desc: Marlee is wondering what mischief he is up to and sends the children off to check. Could he be making her a gift?

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities Of Gold
programme.desc: Tao is now in the hands of Ambrosius. Ambrosius tries to return to his good graces, but Tao sees him as a traitor and refuses to give him his confidence.

programme.title: Bushwhacked!
programme.desc: A matchmaking mission that takes Kayne and Kamil to Lake Eyre and Cooper Pedy, but far from romantic, this adventure involves the world's most venomous snake.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: The Magic Canoe
programme.desc: Julie walks away from the camp without saying where she is going. The other travellers are worried and Max warns her not to go any further. The canoe adventure takes the travellers to the far north. During this expedition, Julie will understand that getting away from her group can be really dangerous!

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: Bob and Rebecca have collected the fragments of a kind of fossil, which they manage to reconstruct and discover it is a mask. Shortly after, our heroes wake up in a strange clearing.

programme.title: The World According to Grandpa
programme.desc: Connie asks 'What are volcanoes?' and Grandpa spins a tale about Peggy the Penguin who dreamed of being a world famous Chilli Chef and set up her restaurant inside a mountain in Hawaii!

programme.title: Chums
programme.desc: The chums gather around the campfire sharing spooky stories after dark.

programme.title: Mysteries Of The Mekong
programme.desc: Chart the seasonal ebb and flow of the Mekong through Cambodia - a nation where everything has a special relationship with water.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Living Black
programme.desc: Karla Grant travels to Western Australia to meet the rappers behind 'Banksia Beats', a hip-hop therapy program that's helping young detainees at Banksia Hill and Unit 18 find their voice through rap.

programme.title: We Don't Need A Map
programme.desc: Filmmaker Warwick Thornton investigates our relationship to the Southern Cross, in this fun and thought provoking ride through Australia's cultural and political landscape.

programme.title: Hidden Volcano: Eruption From The Abyss
programme.desc: January 2022, the volcano Hunga Tonga erupts. It is the most powerful explosion ever recorded. Within minutes waves as high as a six-storey building, devastate the Pacific nation of Tonga.

programme.title: On Country Kitchen
programme.desc: Mark and Derek explore the historic ghost town of Joadja, now a whiskey and gin distillery run by the Jimenez family. Later in Gerringong, they stumble upon a coffee plantation on a picturesque hill.

programme.title: The Cook Up With Adam Liaw
programme.desc: Esteemed chef, Christine Manfield and Enter Via Laundry's founder, Helly Raichura join Adam to celebrate the concept of the communal table - food made for sharing.

programme.title: Spartakus And The Sun Beneath The Sea
programme.desc: The slave Tada was charged with carrying the sacred insignia of his office to the King of Benin. If he accomplishes this assignment, he will be finally a man free.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities Of Gold
programme.desc: The original 80s animation classic that follows a young orphan called Esteban as he searches the New World for both his father and the mysterious Cities of Gold.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities Of Gold
programme.desc: The original 80s animation classic that follows a young orphan called Esteban as he searches the New World for both his father and the mysterious Cities of Gold.

programme.title: Chums
programme.desc: A human girl on Turtle Island befriends a rabbit, a beaver, a ptarmigan as they help a fallen eaglet get back to her nest. They become the \.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: You Haven't Done Nothin' And Christmas Everyday
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of Motown.

programme.title: Harlem Globetrotters: Play It Forward
programme.desc: TNT, Cheese, and Hotshot visit an organization that teaches kids the values of Science, Technology, Engineering, Art, and Math.

programme.title: Garma: Celebrating 25 Years
programme.desc: Join hosts Natalie Ahmat and John Paul Janke for a special two-hour program celebrating 25 years of the iconic Garma Festival, Australia's largest and most significant First Nations cultural event.

programme.title: Indian Country Today News
programme.desc: Indian Country Today News is an independent news channel that serves Indigenous communities with news, entertainment, and opinion.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.sub-title: Noongar Country - The Pinnacles WA Part 2
programme.desc: Explores the stunning land of Noongar Country, with Noongar person Trevor Walley discussing the growth of Aboriginal culture.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Mysteries Of The Mekong
programme.desc: The final country on the Mekong's journey is Vietnam, the home of the river's mighty delta. This area is a true biological treasure trove.

programme.title: Ocean Warriors
programme.desc: The Kitkatla Coastal Nation's crew answer a call for assistance from an injured Sea-Doo operator flipped over by a wave.

programme.title: Ocean Warriors
programme.desc: Kitkatla's Coast Guard Auxiliary Search and Rescue team rush to deal with a Medical Emergency on a runaway tour boat.

programme.title: The Boondock Saints
programme.desc: The ultra-violent, cult-classic thriller set in the depths of Boston's criminal underworld. After inadvertently taking out a pair of Russian Mafiosi, two Irish Catholic brothers believe they have found their calling. Taking justice into their own hands, the pair become vigilantes who fight to cleanse their city of criminals, but hot on their heels is a relentless FBI agent, determined to hunt them down and restore law and order. Starring Norman Reedus, Willem Dafoe and Billy Connolly.

programme.title: Kanehsatake
programme.desc: In July 1990, a dispute over a proposed golf course to be built on Kanien'kéhaka (Mohawk) lands in Oka, Quebec, set the stage for an historic confrontation that would grab international headlines and sear itself into the Canadian consciousness.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: This episode of Bamay showcases beautiful Arrernte and Warlpiri Country, with locations such as Mparntwe Alice Springs and the Ellery Creek Big Hole.

programme.title: Big Backyard Quiz
programme.desc: In this episode of BBQ, team captains Ernie Dingo and Naomi Wenitong compete alongside Lizzy Hoo, Peter Rowsthorn, Luke McGregor and Diana Nguyen, with hosts Narelda Jacobs and Steven Oliver.

programme.title: Nitv On The Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the Easter long weekend. Run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, it's a mixture of Australian and International Indigenous Acts.

programme.title: Nitv On The Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the Easter long weekend. Run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, it's a mixture of Australian and International Indigenous Acts.

programme.title: Nitv On The Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the Easter long weekend. Run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, it's a mixture of Australian and International Indigenous Acts.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.sub-title: North Stradbroke Island, Quandamooka Country Part 1
programme.desc: Slow TV is back on NITV with more beautiful Bamay. Bamay III celebrates great Australian islands and saltwater country. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of country.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.sub-title: North Stradbroke Island, Quandamooka Country Part 1
programme.desc: Slow TV is back on NITV with more beautiful Bamay. Bamay III celebrates great Australian islands and saltwater country. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of country.

programme.title: Toi Time
programme.desc: Tama sells kai at the market, Buzz shows Tama how to make burgers and Aunty Anika explains good manners at the dinner table.

programme.title: Waabiny Time
programme.desc: Maara, hands and djena, feet are very useful to us and together with the other parts of our body help us every day. Maara baam, hands clap and djena kakarook, feet dance. It's too deadly koolangka.

programme.title: Birds Eye View
programme.desc: Big Jim's mirror has gone missing! As much as he hates admitting it, he's missing his sassy cellmate. A bird hunt ensues, with Tui as a self-appointed Sherlock Holmes.

programme.title: Aussie Bush Tales
programme.desc: A mosquito annoys Moort all night. In the morning, he has itchy spots and is in a cranky mood.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities Of Gold
programme.desc: According to the Ambrosius' plan, Laguerra and the soldiers find a deserted village. Laguerra spotts the children hidden in the trees. She hesitates, but leaves without saying anything to her men.

programme.title: Bushwhacked
programme.desc: Kayne and Kamil travel to the Gold Coast in the hope of coming face to face with a predatory Bull Shark. Bull Sharks are responsible for more attacks on humans than any other shark.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: I Want You Back And Nowhere To Run
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of Motown.

programme.title: The Magic Canoe
programme.desc: While Pam is unhappy to be told that she is too small to do anything, Viola sends the campers on a surprise mission!

programme.title: Spartakus And The Sun Beneath The Sea
programme.desc: After Spartakus and the crew lose the pirates they crash into an arctic region.

programme.title: The World According To Grandpa
programme.desc: Stanley asks 'What are rainbows made of?' and Grandpa spins one of his tall tales about Wiswell the Witch who needed to collect seven magical items to make an extra special potion.

programme.title: Chums
programme.desc: Romper is feeling sick, and the Chums try to make her feel better by bringing her different foods and flowers.

programme.title: Mysteries Of The Mekong
programme.desc: The final country on the Mekong's journey is Vietnam, the home of the river's mighty delta. This area is a true biological treasure trove.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Ocean Warriors
programme.desc: The Kitkatla Coastal Nation's crew answer a call for assistance from an injured Sea-Doo operator flipped over by a wave.

programme.title: Ocean Warriors
programme.desc: Kitkatla's Coast Guard Auxiliary Search and Rescue team rush to deal with a Medical Emergency on a runaway tour boat.

programme.title: Manganinnie
programme.desc: Australia, 1830. Edward Waterman and his family arrive in a remote part of Tasmania, known at the time as Van Diemen's Land. Waterman is pressured into helping British colonial forces carry out the Black Line – enforced removal of Aborigines from land near white settlements. Aboriginal woman Manganinnie survives a Black Line raid which claims the life of her husband, Meenopeekameena.

programme.title: Stompem Ground 2022 - Karajarri Dancers
programme.desc: Karajarri dancers are from Mijil Mil Miya, 180 km southwest of Broome. Lead singer Mervyn Mulardy has revived several Karajarri traditional songs inherited from his grandfather and mother.

programme.title: On Country Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Honey, Fritto Misto & Red Wine Tart
programme.desc: Dwayne Bannon-Harrison shares a traditional dance with Mark and Derek. And in Berry, the boys visit a sustainable vineyard.
